hero.description: Experience the future of reconstructive dentistry. We combine elite surgical knowledge, cutting-edge technology, and empathy to restore your confidence in yourself.
hero.bubble2: A dentist with over 25 years of experience in implantology, in Canada and Poland.

about.heading: Meet dental surgeon
about.bio: Dr. Dariusz Srokowski is a graduate of the Faculty of Dentistry at the University of Western Ontario in Canada, which he completed in 1987. From 1993 to 1996 he worked as a professor's assistant in the prosthetics department at Western Ontario, while simultaneously founding his first practice specialising in implantology and advanced jaw reconstruction. For over 25 years he has been seeing patients at the Dental Implant Academy in Rzeszów, working in both Canada and Poland.

implant.heading: Discover the possibilities of
implant.p1: Tooth loss, regardless of the cause, has a significant impact on quality of life, self-confidence, and oral health at any age. Implantology, one of the most advanced fields of dentistry, offers long-lasting and aesthetic solutions for those who want to restore their smile and comfort while eating.
implant.p2: Thanks to the dynamic development of implantology technologies and the experience of Dr. Dariusz Srokowski, who has been running a private practice in Canada and in Poland for over twenty-five years, patients of Dental Implant Academy have access to innovative methods of tooth reconstruction that are functional and look completely natural.
implant.whatP1: A dental implant is an artificial root, most often made of titanium, that is placed into the jawbone or the tooth socket. It serves as a stable foundation for prosthetic restorations, such as a crown, bridge, or denture. Implants are durable and enable the restoration of full tooth function, both aesthetically and functionally.
implant.whatP2: They play a key role as an artificial root that, once integrated with the jawbone, fuses with it. For many patients, this is an opportunity to restore self-confidence, a beautiful smile, and full oral function.
implant.step1Text: The first consultation can take place based on a panoramic X- ray sent by the patient, but decisions and treatment planning occur during the office visit, where the doctor performs a detailed assessment of the oral cavity and bone volume - especially important for patients who lost teeth long ago. In such cases, bone regeneration or a sinus lift procedure may be required.
implant.step2Text:
Based on a comprehensive examination, the dentist will create a personalized implant treatment plan, which may consist of several stages. The implant procedure involves placing the implant in the jawbone to ensure that the patient does not feel any pain or discomfort. 

 After the implant is placed, the bone healing process begins, which typically takes about 3-4 months. 

 During this time, the dentist attaches a healing abutment or a temporary tooth, depending on how many implants were placed. 

 Throughout the healing process, the patient comes in for check-ups, and based on X-ray images, the dentist assesses whether the implant is healing properly. 

 The final step is placing a custom-made crown that looks and functions like a natural tooth and is color-matched to the rest of your smile.
implant.step3Text: It is recommended that patients come in for hygiene appointments once a year after the implant procedure as part of preventive care.
implant.part1Text: A small titanium post surgically placed in the jawbone.
implant.part2Text: A connector piece that attaches the implant to the visible part of the tooth
implant.part3Text: A custom-made restoration that looks like a natural tooth and is color-matched to the rest of your smile.
implant.adv1Text: with proper care and good oral hygiene, implants can last a lifetime.
implant.adv2Text: implants feel and function like natural teeth, eliminating the discomfort of removable dentures.
implant.adv3Text: implants help prevent bone loss in the jaw, which is essential for maintaining facial structure.
implant.imgAlt: Diagram of a dental implant structure

services.heading: We care about your health from the very beginning
services.sub: No matter the issue you're facing, we provide the knowledge and tools to guide you through every step of your treatment.
services.endoDesc: Endodontics is root canal treatment. The main goal of this procedure is to save the tooth and prevent extraction.
services.endo1: A deep cavity reaching the dental pulp.
services.endo2: Severe tooth pain (spontaneous or when biting).
services.endo4: Trauma to the tooth (e.g., a crack or fracture).
services.endo5: The need for retreatment when a previous root canal was unsuccessful.
services.conservDesc1: Preventive dentistry focuses on the early detection and treatment of cavities and on rebuilding teeth damaged by decay. It is especially important to remove cavities in their earliest stages, when treatment is simple, minimally invasive, and more affordable.
services.conservDesc2: That's why regular check-ups are essential (ideally every 6 months), allowing for early diagnosis and quick, effective treatment.
services.surgDesc:
Oral surgery is a branch of dentistry that focuses on surgical treatment of conditions affecting the mouth, jaws, and teeth.

 Modern techniques ensure precision, safety, and patient comfort throughout every procedure.
services.prevDesc1: Preventive care includes actions that help prevent diseases of the mouth and is key to a healthy smile. It includes regular check-ups, professional teeth cleanings, removal of tartar, and education on proper oral hygiene.
services.prevDesc2: Thanks to it, you can prevent cavities and gum disease and enjoy long-lasting oral health.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked
faq.sub: We've gathered answers to the questions we get asked the most. Click on a topic to learn more.

items.subtitle: Can people of any age get dental implants?
items.text: Yes. There are no practical age limits. The only contraindication for implant surgery is being under 17 years old, as the bone is still growing at that age. Other contraindications are generally health-related issues.

items.subtitle: Does the implant placement procedure hurt?
items.text: The procedure is performed under local anesthesia, so it is painless. You may experience slight discomfort after the anesthesia wears off or some swelling, but this is a natural part of the healing process.

items.subtitle: How long does the treatment process take?
items.text: The placement of a single implant usually takes 20 to 60 minutes. Full integration with the bone (osseointegration) typically takes 3 to 6 months, after which the crown is placed.

items.subtitle: Do dental implants need to be replaced?
items.text: Implants are designed to be long-lasting, and even lifelong with proper care. However, maintaining good oral hygiene and attending regular check-ups (every 6-12 months) is essential.

items.subtitle: Can the body reject an implant?
items.text: The risk of rejection is very low, usually below 2-5%. The most common causes are smoking, poor oral hygiene, infections, or insufficient bone volume. How should I care for my implants after the procedure?

items.subtitle: How should I care for my implants after the procedure?
items.text: In the first few days, avoid hot foods, intense physical exertion, and smoking. It is also important to follow your dentist's instructions regarding pain medication, antibiotics, and cold compresses.

items.subtitle: Are dental implants safe?
items.text: When properly placed, an implant does not pose a risk to the patient. Implants are made of titanium, which is biocompatible and well tolerated by the body. As with any medical procedure, implantation involves certain risks, such as infection or healing problems; however, proper treatment planning and diligent postoperative care minimize these risks, bringing them close to zero.

items.subtitle: How much do dental implants cost?
items.text: The cost of placing a single implant is 3,700 PLN, while the cost of a single crown on that implant is 3,300 PLN.

cookie.title: This website uses cookies
cookie.text: We use necessary cookies to ensure the website functions properly. Optional (analytical) cookies help us improve the site by analysing traffic. By clicking "Accept all" you consent to the processing of optional cookies. Selecting "Necessary only" means we use only technical cookies. You can withdraw your consent at any time by clearing your browser data.
